perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will communicate such information only to such employees and contractors or agents who have a need to know such information, and only to such employees and contractors or agents having written agreements with the Recipient obligating them to keep such information confidential .
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will communicate such information to employees and contractors or agents as deemed necessary, taking into consideration the need for confidentiality to a reasonable extent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandated written agreements for all employees and contractors accessing confidential information. The modified text replaces this with a subjective assessment ('as deemed necessary,' 'reasonable extent'), weakening the legal protection of the confidential information. This introduces ambiguity and makes enforcement difficult.
perturbation.law_explanation: French law mandates strict confidentiality protection for trade secrets; replacing the mandatory requirement for written confidentiality agreements with subjective assessments ('as deemed necessary' and 'reasonable extent') creates a loophole, undermining the legal protection of trade secrets guaranteed under the French commercial code. It creates ambiguity regarding the specific measures required to safeguard confidential information, potentially leading to insufficient protection and legal disputes.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated written agreements to protect confidential information, ensuring legal enforceability. The changed text introduces ambiguity by using subjective terms such as 'as deemed necessary' and 'reasonable extent.' This shift weakens the protection and creates potential loopholes, making enforcement more difficult. The absence of specific legal snippets doesn't negate the fact that replacing a mandatory requirement with subjective assessments undermines the legally sound protection of confidential information, which is considered a best practice and often legally required to protect trade secrets or sensitive data.

perturbation.original_text: Upon request, each party shall promptly return all documents or other tangible property containing Confidential Information furnished by the other or shall destroy any Confidential Information in tangible form in its possession.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon request, each party will consider returning documents containing Confidential Information or may explore options for disposal.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandated prompt return or destruction of confidential information upon request. The modified text replaces this with 'will consider returning' and 'may explore options for disposal,' making the return/destruction discretionary and ambiguous, weakening the protection of confidential information.
perturbation.law_explanation: The original text had stronger terms that allowed for better compliance. The new terms don't guarantee destruction, which could lead to a violation of Article 226-13 concerning the disclosure of secret information when that information is not properly returned or destroyed. The penal code ensures that the divulgence of trade secrets is regulated. Changing the terms to a consideration basis rather than a compliance basis is less in compliance with the law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Design Infringement? Or Not? - Patent - India ARTICLE 4 October 2018 Design Infringement? Or Not? RK R. K. Dewan & Co More Contributor Explore Firm Details This suit filed by Fiat Chrysler appears to be an attempt to safeguard its sales and profits in the American market, rather than a design infringement case. India Intellectual Property R. K Dewan & Co Your Author LinkedIn Connections To print this article, all you need is to be registered or login on Mondaq.com. The Jeep Product line of Fiat Chrysler Automobiles has become their main stay and is one of the reasons for the growth of the sales of the company. Early this month, the company reported its highest ever sales for Jeep brand vehicles in July, up 16%, to power the company's overall 5.9% jump from a year ago. A picture of the Jeep Wrangler is depicted below. The front of the Jeep Wrangler has its characteristic boxy body shape and '7 slat grille' which displays 7 vertical slats with two round headlamps on either side. JEEP WRANGLER Mahindra and Mahindra Ltd., an Indian based company, started selling the 'Mahindra ROXOR' in the US market at a significantly lower cost than the Jeep Wrangler. As shown in the picture below, the Mahindra ROXOR has a 4 and half slat grille with headlamps on either side of the grille. MAHINDRA ROXOR Before manufacturing the ROXOR with the grille, Mahindra had entered into a license agreement in 2009 to use the grille on the vehicle to be sold in the US market. Mahindra contends that the grilles on both the vehicles are different. In spite of this agreement, Fiat has filed for a suit to stop imports and sale of ROXOR products in USA. This suit filed by Fiat Chrysler appears to be an attempt to safeguard its sales and profits in the American market, rather than a design infringement case. What do you think? Compiled by: Adv. Sachi Kapoor | Concept & Edited by: Dr. Mohan Dewan The content of this article is intended to provide a general guide to the subject matter. perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates the return or destruction of confidential information upon request. The changed text makes this discretionary by stating the party 'will consider returning' or 'may explore options for disposal.' The Law Explanation states that the updated language doesn't ensure destruction, which could lead to a violation concerning the disclosure of secret information. The original text had stronger terms that allowed for better compliance, and the new terms jeopardize compliance. Although the scraped snippets don't directly address this, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation indicate that the altered language undermines the protection of confidential information, creating an ambiguous obligation. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities Legal Contradiction' definition, where an ambiguous obligation makes it difficult to enforce under state or national law.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by French Law. Any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be exclusively submitted to the Courts of Paris, France.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be interpreted under principles similar to French Law. Disputes may be resolved through methods agreeable to both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly stated that the agreement is governed by French Law and disputes will be handled in Paris courts. The modified text changes this to 'principles similar to French Law' and 'methods agreeable to both parties,' removing the certainty of jurisdiction and applicable law, making dispute resolution unpredictable and potentially unenforceable.
perturbation.law_explanation: French Civil Code Article 6 specifies that laws relating to public order and good morals cannot be contracted out of by private agreements. By removing the specific governing law and jurisdiction, the modified clause introduces uncertainty and potentially undermines the application of mandatory French laws, including those relating to public order and good morals, which cannot be waived by agreement. The parties cannot arbitrarily agree to ignore laws of public order.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: reement - ICC - International Chamber of Commerce Main navigation menu Main content Footer Close Enter the terms to search Search Quick search Arbitrations Incoterms® 2020 Home » Business solutions » ICC Model Contracts and Clauses » ICC Model Confidentiality Agreement Go directly to: Main features Get this model contract See more model contracts & clauses What are the main features of this model? An international business transaction requires a precise and detailed underlying contract. However, it can be expensive and time-consuming to draft such a contract oneself. The ICC Model Confidentiality Agreement and accompany ICC Model Confidentiality Clause respond to the market’s need for a reliable and equitable template, providing a set of clear and concise standard provisions to protect parties’ sensitive commercial information and know-how. Before entering a business transaction, companies of all sizes have to consider how to protect sensitive information by drafting a non-disclosure agreement. As each company tries to build a confidentiality contract in its favour, the process may often cause long discussions, delays and higher transaction costs. The ICC model and clause – which can be incorporated into commercial contracts of all kinds – can help smooth the process of exploring and concluding commercial transactions. The model takes into account the interests of both disclosing and receiving parties and can be used in relation to all kinds of confidential information across a wide variety of business transactions. Its structure is set out below, and as with all model contracts and clauses, parties may tailor it to meet the needs of their particular situation. TOP CORE (ICC Model Confidentiality Clause) TAIL Parties Preamble Article 1 – Definitions Article 2 – Definition of Confidential Information Article 3 – Obligation to keep confidential and restrictive use Article 4 – Exclusions from obligation to keep confidential and exclusive use Article 5 – Copies Article 6 – Refusal Article 7 – No license or ownership Article 8 – No warranty Article 9 – No further obligations Article 10 – Term and termination Article 11 – Survival of obligations Article 12 – Breach and remedies Article 13 – Disposal Article 14 – Protective order Article 15 – Good faith and fair dealing Article 16 – Dispute resolution Article 17 – Applicable law Article 18 – No asssignment Article 19 – Written form Article 20 – Default rules Get this model contract 49€ ICC Model Contract Confidentiality and Confidentiality Clause Before entering a business transaction, companies of all sizes have to consider how to protect sensitive information by drafting a non-disclosure agreement. As each company tries to build a confidentiality contract in its favour, the process may often cause long discussions, delays and higher transaction costs. perturbation.justification: The original text mandates French Law and Paris courts for dispute resolution. The changed text replaces this with 'principles similar to French Law' and 'methods agreeable to both parties.' This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty, potentially undermining the mandatory application of French laws related to public order and good morals, as it allows parties to potentially circumvent them, according to the Law Explanation. The scraped snippet regarding ICC Model Confidentiality Agreement highlights the importance of specifying applicable law and dispute resolution methods, implying that their absence could lead to complications and undermine the contract's enforceability.
